title: Welcome to Linux!
description: Meet your new desktop and discover what makes Linux special. You're a penguin now! 🐧

steps.instruction: Welcome to Linux! 🐧 See the dark bar at the top? That's the Top Bar. Click the 'Activities' button in the top-left corner to see what happens!
steps.hint: Look at the very top-left of the screen — there's an 'Activities' button. Click it!

steps.instruction: See the vertical dock on the left side? That's your app launcher! Click the Files icon (📁) to open the file manager.
steps.hint: The dock is the column of icons on the left side of the screen. Click the folder icon (📁) at the top.

steps.instruction: Nice! You've opened the Files app (also known as Nautilus). Now close it by clicking the ✕ button on the right side of the title bar.
steps.hint: Look at the top-right corner of the Files window. Click the ✕ button to close it.

steps.instruction: Now right-click anywhere on the empty desktop. In Linux, this gives you quick access to useful actions!
steps.hint: Move your mouse to any empty spot on the desktop background and right-click.
